name: solstice-codex
description: Built-in AI agent panel powered by Codex (ChatGPT subscription).

enumDescriptions: GPT-5.5 via the codex CLI (ChatGPT subscription) — full agent
Claude Code via the claude CLI — DISABLED unless solstice.codex.allowClaude is true
Grok 4.3 Build via the grok CLI — agentic fallback
Composer 2.5 Fast via the grok CLI — fast builder
solstice.codex.provider.description: Which model drives the Solstice agent. Default is composer-2.5 (Thomas's directive). Claude is gated behind solstice.codex.allowClaude and will not run unless that flag is explicitly enabled.

solstice.codex.allowClaude.description: Explicit opt-in gate for the Claude provider. OFF by default so Claude never runs unattended. When false, selecting 'claude' falls back to gpt-5.5 and the Claude option is hidden from the model picker. Set to true only when you deliberately want Claude to drive the agent.

solstice.codex.groqApiKey.description: Groq API key for voice dictation (Whisper transcription) in the agent composer. Empty = falls back to the GROQ_API_KEY environment variable.

solstice.codex.dictationLanguage.description: Language hint for voice dictation transcription (ISO-639-1, e.g. 'he', 'en'). Use 'auto' to let Whisper detect the language.

solstice.codex.path.description: Path to the codex binary. Empty = bundled binary, then PATH.

solstice.codex.home.description: CODEX_HOME directory for auth/config. Empty = codex default (~/.codex).

solstice.codex.approvalPolicy.description: When the agent must ask before running commands.

enumDescriptions: Supervised — ask before every file edit, command, and tool call (safest).
Auto-edit — apply file edits and reads automatically, but ask before running shell commands and external tools.
Autonomous — fully trust the agent: approve everything automatically and never interrupt.
solstice.codex.autonomy.description: How much the agent can do without asking. Switch live from the agent panel (shield icon) or 'Solstice: Set Agent Autonomy'. 'Autonomous' = the 'I trust the agent, run on its own' mode. Legacy approvalPolicy='never' also forces full autonomy.

solstice.codex.claudePath.description: Path to the claude binary (Claude Code CLI). Empty = `claude` from PATH.

solstice.codex.claudePermissionMode.description: Permission mode passed to the claude CLI. In headless --print mode tools that would prompt are denied; raise to bypassPermissions if shell commands get blocked.

solstice.codex.designElevation.description: Design Elevation layer (OPTIONAL, off by default). When on, the premium design playbook (sector-fit, 10-15 sections, real imagery, GSAP/R3F, reference deconstruction, self-verify) is injected into the agent's instructions on every build. Toggle via 'Solstice: Toggle Design Elevation'. Both the user and the agent can switch it to compare the same build with/without the high-design layer.

solstice.codex.projectsDir.description: Root directory the Projects Gallery scans for projects built on this server. Empty = auto-detect (~/solstice-deploys, then ~/Projects). Each immediate subfolder that looks like a project (package.json / index.html / .git / .solstice) becomes a gallery card.

solstice.codex.galleryServerUrl.description: Base URL of the remote Solstice Gallery server (the build server's ~/solstice-deploys, exposed over Tailscale). When set, the Projects gallery lists the projects agents built on that server and opens each as a live site. Empty = local filesystem scan only.

solstice.codex.fleetDir.description: Root of the fleet agent workspaces, used by the in-IDE Fleet panel to talk to Orion/Jasper/Asher. Empty = auto-detect (~/Julius-cc-x/agents). Messages are dropped into each agent's inbox; the agent's reply channel is mirrored back into the panel.

solstice.fleet.token.description: Shared bearer token for the live WebSocket bridge to fleet agents. Empty = read from ~/.solstice/fleet-token. Required for any agent listed in solstice.fleet.bridges.

solstice.fleet.bridges.description: Fleet agents reachable over a live WebSocket straight to their brain (SolsticeBridgeChannel on the server, over Tailscale). Each entry: {id, name, role, glyph, model, wsUrl, token?}. Agents listed here use the live socket; others fall back to the file-drop inbox.

wsUrl.description: ws:// or wss:// URL of the agent's bridge

token.description: Per-agent token override; falls back to solstice.fleet.token

solstice.liveness.enabled.description: Progress-aware liveness model for the Solstice builder. Unlike the wall-clock watchdog, it only counts REAL output (token deltas, streaming text, file/tool events) as 'alive'. A busy animation with no strong signal degrades alive→quiet→stalled, so a hung turn can't masquerade as working for hours.

solstice.liveness.freshSeconds.description: Seconds since the last strong output signal under which the build is shown as 'alive' (green). Above this it goes 'quiet' (amber).

solstice.liveness.stallSeconds.description: Seconds with no strong output signal after which the build is flagged 'stalled' (red) and recovery-by-resume is offered — drains any queued steering messages and nudges the agent instead of a blind restart.

solstice.atrium.clientsDir.description: Root of the Atrium client folders that finished Solstice builds are handed off into (one subfolder per client, e.g. kobi/tali/moshiko). Empty = auto-detect ~/Julius-cc-x/agents/atrium/output. Handoff writes <clientsDir>/<client>/solstice/<project>/handoff.json plus a copy of the build when source and clients dir share a filesystem.

solstice.atrium.baseUrl.description: Base URL of the Atrium app (e.g. http://localhost:5180 or the deployed URL). When set, the Solstice→Atrium handoff panel deep-links the client card at <baseUrl>/clients/<client>. Empty = no deep-link (folder handoff still works).

solstice.fleet.hidden.description: Agent ids hidden from the Fleet roster. Built-in agents (orion/jasper/asher) removed via the Fleet panel's × button are recorded here; add an id back to the roster by removing it from this list.
